item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The plane demands a sacrifice
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well THAT went from 0 to crazy real fast. Fun read. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This wasn't a direct reference to the United Airline incident (as in this is not supposed to be symbolic of it or anything), but it was partially inspired by it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure where the idea is originated from. But are you refering to the United Airline overbooked shit that happened months ago?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
yet another SCP that creates pod people

I did not realize this was an SCP trope until you pointed it out… huh. Learn something new every day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was enjoying an interesting buildup of two things that go well together (mob mentality and the claustrophobia of flying) but was left disappointed by neither of these things being really explored in favour of yet another SCP that creates pod people. Novoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought about doing that for a little bit, but I decided it was just easier to state it instead of having people get caught up on the "but that's not 100%!" even if I do explain it later. Don't want to pull people out of the read, you know?

Thanks for the upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
See Addendum SCP-3767-1 for details on the remaining case.

I feel that this would be better implied and not explicitly stated (for those who notice that the two percentages don't quite add up). Otherwise, solid job. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I mean, 100 seemed like a good number. You could also head canon they rounded so its only "about 100" and that last case is "about 1%". I didn't think too much about the exact number tbh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In 78% of recorded cases, the passengers collectively decide on an individual to remove, who is then ejected through the airlock. In 21% of cases, an individual removes themselves from the plane. See Addendum SCP-3767-1 for details on the remaining case.

Wouldn't this imply that they have recorded this happening exactly 100 times? Not that this couldn't be the case, mind you, or that it detracts from the article in any way. It just kind of struck me as oddly precise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was a really good read, build-up of tension was great and I can always appreciate a nice twist that still leaves a good bit to the imagination. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And you wonder why some people drink before they get on a flight…

Response + Spoilers
I guess...

Your description is pretty much what I was going for. The collapsible is just to safeguard against anyone who doesn't want this confirmed.

The only issue is, at 1 meter per second, they should have had 3 hours before crashing, which does not seem like the amount of time that passed.

I wanted to imply that the camera cut out because it was hit, and turned off, or unplugged, or something to that effect. Either way, the camera cuts out before the plane crashes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty fucking wild.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of SCP-342

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, I thought it was pretty clear.

First, an extra passenger gets put on board. This passenger is a fake person.

In almost all cases, when there isn't outside interference, someone gets thrown off the plane. The plane then ascends, and lands safely. If the passenger that got thrown off was the fake person, no issue. Otherwise (63% of the time) a real person has gone missing… And fake person goes on living somewhere, presumably. And in all cases of someone being thrown off, there is no second plane.

In the particular case, they did not throw anyone off the plane before hitting the ground. All the real people, and the fake kid, died. New plane was created. The fact the d-3114 from the new plane was fake, implies EVERYONE on the new plane is fake. Which is also implied by all of the real versions being dead in the plane that hit the ground.

The only issue is, at 1 meter per second, they should have had 3 hours before crashing, which does not seem like the amount of time that passed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a pretty neat scenario, like, are we willing to collectively throw someone out an airlock to save ourselves? And then you get to that very final line and um uh no thank you this is not okay D: +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So this is a really hecking weird and hecking scary article, and it reminds me of a terrifying time I had when I was on a plane from Shanghai to… Beijing? Was visiting family and we had to take some weird connecting flights.

The plane encountered turbulence, but it was more of erratic kangaroo bouncing than the "plane shaking a bit" that I usually associate with turbulence, and it freaked me out. The memory still makes me shake a bit. This plays off those fears and sticks in my head afterward. There's plenty of lingering mystery that makes for some unsettling answers to the questions I have.

Take your +1 :V

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I always has to end in a twist, doesn't it?
But it makes no sense at all. We know for a fact that there were 2 planes. In the crashed plane the kid was fake, nobody got thrown out and it crashed killing everybody on board including the kid and D-3114. In plane 2 D-3114 was fake, and concisely tried to hide the fact he has pretzel inside him by asking "who here is eating". But nobody got thrown out, and the plane landed successfully.
Which means that either the SCP is playing with the foolish humans trying to explore its nature or 2 realities got switched at the moment the camera broke(D-3144 was fake on plane 1 and the kid on plane 2 but they switched places), which still doesn't explain why plane 2 landed successfully(except if they didn't throw out the real kid and the foundation missed that little detail)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it! Just the right amount of bizarreness, and an overall enjoyable read.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's my interpretation

The plane goes to hell (thus the sky being red) and splits into two. One plane returns to our world with all the passengers inside dead and the other stays in hell and doesn't die. However, some flights can return to our world with everyone surviving. The plane the Class D was on just so happened to be a surviving plane and a dead plane arrived too.

Also, +1 just for that pic alone it's very /comfy/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I always knew there was something anomalous related to Newark Internation Airport.

+1 for some fun weirdness (those last lines were also great).

item.title: Would You Like Some Complementary Snacks?
item.content:encoded:
So, I wrote this on a flight because my computer was dying and I got bored of the games on my phone. Then, on my connecting flight I was put in an exit row. So… that was neat.

Thanks to CadaverCommander, Weryllium, Mortos, Zyn, and taylor_itkin for looking this over.

If you want to check out more of my stuff you can find it here.

Have a nice flight!
